mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but may require careful resource management and configuration for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the llm-d command-line interface to manage deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

llm-d version

```

> Download the latest CLI version.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.
> Verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --num-nodes=3 --cluster-version=1.31 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Ensure that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the status of the nodes.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
Deploy llm-d using Helm to manage the application lifecycle.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Install llm-d in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for llm-d.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/examples/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
Remove llm-d and all associated resources. This may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the llm-d release.
> Delete the Kubernetes cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Backup existing configurations, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade llm-d to the latest version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust configurations as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the llm-d deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster cannot pull the image due to authentication issues.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the image pull secrets are correctly configured.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the Running state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and sufficient resources before proceeding.
